Comment: This class is hard. Average on midterm was a 46%, could revise for half of what was lost. Labeled as a beginner course, but soon after midterm became far too complex for even the best students. Professor can be nice but is blunt. Class style is odd, most content written by hand, minimal demonstration online. Homework 1.5+ hour each week. Can use AI
Tags: Tough grader, Lots of homework, Lecture heavy

Comment: The course starts out fine enough, but pretty quickly delves into material too difficult for an introductory coding class. The professor is also not very helpful with questions, and "attendance" is more like a participation grade, so stay engaged. He also seems to hold different standards for some students, so stay on his good side if you can.

More difficult than I thought going in, students with prior coding experience excelled. That being said, he gives you a lot of material to read and ask questions over, and there are a ton of grades in the class, so a couple bombed assignments wont hurt you too much. Decent guy, but comes off as rude to some people

Ineffective teaching style, issues with tone when speaking with students, and does not respond to emails. Unless you're good at teaching yourself or you already have experience with Python, it's not worth the trouble.
Tags: Participation matters, Lots of homework, Lecture heavy

Comment: avoid taking him at any cost.
